video:video.video:title: Austrian drag queens brave record temperatures at annual drag sports event
video:video.video:description: Athletes on Saturday braved record temperatures at a drag queen spoof sporting event in Vienna. Temperatures in the Austrian capital soared past 38 degrees Celsius (100.4 Fahenheit) breaking an earlier record from 2012.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 27
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, June 27th: Multiple wildfires burn through Utah; Hezbollah criticizes Israel-Lebanon framework deal; Hungarians celebrate first Pride march in Budapest since Viktor Orbán was ousted; Thousands of Vespas swarm Rome for 80th anniversary.

video:video.video:title: Could Wimbledon's new AI app features replace sports commentators?
video:video.video:description: From AI generated match analysis to a chatbot that can answer questions about players and live matches, Wimbledon has been expanding the role of AI across its app and website.

video:video.video:title: US Central Command releases video of strikes on Iran in second day of attacks
video:video.video:description: U.S. Central Command released video early Sunday purporting to show strikes on Iranian military targets.

video:video.video:title: Sirens sound over Kuwait as Iran targets country with drones and missiles
video:video.video:description: Sirens sounded in Kuwait City early on Sunday after both Bahrain and Kuwait said Iran targeted their nations with drone and missile fire.

video:video.video:title: AP footage captures Israeli strikes hitting central Gaza on Saturday
video:video.video:description: AP footage captured the moment that Israeli strikes hit Deir al Balah, in Central Gaza Strip on Saturday evening. No casualties were immediately reported.

video:video.video:title: Residents in Lebanon and Israel react to framework agreement with U.S.
video:video.video:description: Residents in Lebanon and Israel react to framework agreement with U.S.

video:video.video:title: Thousands of Vespa riders join Rome parade as iconic scooter turns 80
video:video.video:description: Over 10,000 Vespas moved around the Colosseum and past the Roman Forum on Saturday, marking the 80th anniversary of the iconic scooter as enthusiasts gathered from continental Europe, northern England, San Francisco, Australia’s Gold Coast, the Philippines and more.

video:video.video:title: Desperate Venezuelans search quake rubble as anger grows over response
video:video.video:description: Desperation set in on Saturday among Venezuelans trying to locate their loved ones alive among piles of rubble, where public housing buildings and ocean view towers stood until twin powerful earthquakes brought them down midweek.

video:video.video:title: Fans celebrate as Egypt progresses to World Cup knockout rounds
video:video.video:description: Egypt soccer fans celebrated from Seattle to Cairo and in the camps of the Gaza Strip as the Pharaohs kept Iran to a 1-1 draw in their final Group B game in the World Cup advancing Egypt past the group stage at the World Cup for the first time.

video:video.video:title: Iraqis upset as big loss to Senegal seals World Cup exit
video:video.video:description: Iraqi soccer fans expressed their shock and disappointment after watching their team suffer its third heavy defeat in the World Cup after a 10-man Iraqi side was eliminated without a point in its first World Cup appearance since 1986 following their 5-0 defeat by Senegal in its final group stage mat

video:video.video:title: Vespa drivers join Rome parade as iconic scooter turns 80
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Vespas buzzed through Rome on Saturday, celebrating the scooter's 80th anniversary. Enthusiasts from Europe, the US, Australia and the Philippines gathered to honor the iconic brand.

video:video.video:title: Cape Verde fans in joyous celebrations outside stadium after making World Cup knockout round
video:video.video:description: Cape Verde fans celebrated outside Houston Stadium on Friday evening, after making the World Cup knockout round.

video:video.video:title: Tropical Storm Mekkhala continues to drench Japan with heavy downpours
video:video.video:description: The heavy rain caused disruptions to the country's railway network, prompting severe delays and service suspensions in various regions.

video:video.video:title: Iranians celebrate World Cup draw but must wait to see if team progresses
video:video.video:description: Young supporters celebrated in Tehran early on Saturday after Iran drew 1-1 against Egypt in the World Cup tournament as Team Melli will have to wait a day to find out if they will make it to the next round of the soccer tournament after the Iranians scored a potential late winning goal, but an offs

video:video.video:title: Rainbow flags dot Iran protest in Seattle ahead of World Cup match
video:video.video:description: Demonstrators took to the streets of Seattle on Friday amid the World Cup match between Egypt and Iran.

video:video.video:title: Cape Verdeans celebrate as tiny nation reaches World Cup knockout round
video:video.video:description: Supporters danced and celebrated as the small island nation of Cape Verde completed an improbable run through the group stage with a third straight World Cup draw to become the smallest country to earn a spot in the knockout as it makes it's debut on soccer's grandest stage.

video:video.video:title: Wildfire burns through southern Utah pushed by strong winds also impeding firefighting efforts
video:video.video:description: Large plumes of smoke were seen in various locations in Utah after a large wildfire expanded its reach across tinder-dry forests as crews rush to fight new blazes in the arid state.

video:video.video:title: Analysts assess how and if Andy Burnham will forge different path for UK than Keir Starmer
video:video.video:description: Andy Burnham, the former mayor of Greater Manchester who was sworn into Parliament hours after Keir Starmer announced his resignation on Monday, will be constrained by the platform the center-left Labour Party was elected on that decisively ended 14 years of Conservative rule in 2024.

video:video.video:title: Video shows panic in immediate aftermath of double earthquake in Venezuela's capital Caracas
video:video.video:description: Footage showed panic in the streets of Caracas as back-to-back earthquakes struck the Venezuelan capital.

video:video.video:title: Largest wildfire in the US burns through forests in Utah
video:video.video:description: The largest fire in the United States is burning its way though the mountains of Utah

video:video.video:title: Croatia fans marching through Philadelphia crash a wedding party
video:video.video:description: Croatian fans took to the streets of Philadelphia on Friday carrying a giant national flag stretching more than 100 yards, ahead of Croatia's final FIFA World Cup group-stage match against Ghana.

video:video.video:title: Araghchi insists Iran will oversee reopening Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Sunday insisted his country would oversee the reopening and continued operation of the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Serbia’s populist President Vucic says he will resign within weeks as election nears
video:video.video:description: Serbia's populist President Aleksandar Vucic said Saturday he will resign the post within weeks, apparently to pave the way for early elections following youth-led protests.

video:video.video:title: Soccer-mad Bangladeshis cheer on Argentina all the way from Dhaka
video:video.video:description: Waving Argentine flags and wearing the team’s famous striped jersey, Bangladeshi fans cheered as the team of Lionel Messi beat Jordan 3-1 to complete a sweep of their group.

video:video.video:title: Emergency services in attendance after skydiving plane crashes in northeastern France, killing all 11 on board
video:video.video:description: Emergency services in attendance after skydiving plane crashes in northeastern France, killing all 11 on board

video:video.video:title: Pope reiterates support for quake-hit Venezuela, prays for victims
video:video.video:description: Pope reiterates support for quake-hit Venezuela, prays for victims

video:video.video:title: Frustration grows as nearly 70,000 still missing in deadly Venezuela earthquakes
video:video.video:description: Tensions flared Saturday as desperation grew in Venezuela's state of La Guaira as rescuers and civilians searched for earthquake survivors and the death toll rose sharply to 1,430.

video:video.video:title: Mexico, U.S. reopen sterile fly plant to fight screwworm as outbreak disrupts cattle trade
video:video.video:description: Mexico and the United States reopened Saturday a sterile fly production facility in the southern Mexican state of Chiapas, expanding efforts to contain the New World screwworm, a livestock pest that has disrupted cross-border cattle trade.

video:video.video:title: Croatia and Ghana soccer fans celebrate together as both teams advance in World Cup
video:video.video:description: Croatia and Ghana fans celebrated together outside Philadelphia Stadium as they danced and waved their national flags. (AP video by Tassanee Vejpongsa)

video:video.video:title: Celebrations in Kansas City and Algiers as Algeria advance at World Cup
video:video.video:description: Celebrations took place in Kansas City and Algiers as Algeria advanced at the World Cup following their match against Austria on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Fans react to England beating Panama 2-0 to win World Cup group
video:video.video:description: Rain couldn't dampen the spirits of fans attending the England versus Panama match at the MetLife Stadium in New Jersey on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Thousands join Pride celebrations across Latin America
video:video.video:description: Members of the LGBTQ+ community across Latin America celebrated Pride on Saturday with parades, music and dancing.

video:video.video:title: Luke Combs, Lainey Wilson and other stars celebrate Alan Jackson at his final concert
video:video.video:description: Country Hall of Famer Alan Jackson hung up his hat with a final concert in Nashville, Tennessee. Lainey Wilson, Luke Combs and many more came out to support him.

video:video.video:title: Riding the waves on Ireland's wild Atlantic coast
video:video.video:description: Ireland’s huge Atlantic waves combined with improved surfing infrastructure and a growing online reputation have transformed the country's coast into a fast growing international tourist attraction.

video:video.video:title: Winds hamper crews as fast-moving fire in Utah burns through forests
video:video.video:description: The largest fire in the United States has spread through tens of thousands of square miles of tinder-dry forest in Utah, fueled by conditions that fire managers and experts call unprecedented.

video:video.video:title: Panini sticker swapping an 'iconic part of any World Cup'
video:video.video:description: The stickers depicting players and teams in the World Cup have been around since 1970. More than 50 years later, fans young and old swap stickers to help each other fill their keepsake albums.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 28
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, June 28th: Iran attacks Bahrain and Kuwait in response to US airstrikes; Teams scramble to find survivors four days after Venezuela quakes; Skydiving plane crash kills 11 in France; Thousands march in celebration of Pride in NYC.

video:video.video:title: Czech Republic records hottest day ever as heatwave moves east
video:video.video:description: Czech Republic records hottest day ever as heatwave moves east

video:video.video:title: Albanians keep protesting against Kushner-linked resort project
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Albanians continued to protest against a coastal development project linked to Donald Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner, on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Russian strike kills two and injures 17 in Zaporizhzhia
video:video.video:description: Russian strike kills two and injures 17 in Zaporizhzhia

video:video.video:title: Allison Janney says acting helps her to build a legacy
video:video.video:description: Allison Janney says joining the world of "Minions & Monsters" is about more than just landing another role. (June 28)

video:video.video:title: Venezuela earthquake rescue operation approaches 96-hour mark with thousands still missing
video:video.video:description: Local and international rescue teams raced against the clock to pull survivors from the rubble in Venezuela on Sunday, four days after two powerful earthquakes shook the northern state of La Guaira.

video:video.video:title: Mother and 18-day-old baby rescued from rubble of building 32 hours after Venezuela earthquakes
video:video.video:description: A mother and her 18-day-old baby survived for 32 hours after Venezuela's devastating double earthquake before they were rescued from underneath the rubble.

video:video.video:title: US rescue workers pull father and son from rubble in La Guaira, Venezuela after deadly quakes
video:video.video:description: United States search and rescue team members pulled a father and his son from the rubble on Sunday, four days after Venezuela powerful quakes. (AP video by Juan Arraez.)

video:video.video:title: Preliminary agreement between US and Iran appears to be unravelling after new exchanges
video:video.video:description: Iran again launched drone and missile attacks Sunday targeting Bahrain and Kuwait in response to new U.S. airstrikes against the Islamic Republic, and threatened a “complete halt” in negotiations to end the war if Washington continues its attacks.

video:video.video:title: US rescuers pull survivors from Venezuela quake rubble
video:video.video:description: United States search and rescue team members pulled a father and his young son from the rubble on Sunday in Venezuela's state of La Guaira, days after two powerful earthquakes hit the country.

video:video.video:title: Forensic workers inspect wreckage after plane crash in northeast France kills 11
video:video.video:description: Families watched in shock as a skydiving plane carrying their loved ones on what was meant to be a thrilling introduction to parachuting crashed in northeastern France on Sunday, killing all 11 people on board, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Rift Valley's famous pink flamingos fade to white as their food source is threatened
video:video.video:description: The future of the famous Rift Valley flamingos is becoming uncertain as the ecology of lakes they rely on are changing.

video:video.video:title: Iran video said to show missiles being launched in response to US strikes
video:video.video:description: Tehran launched drone and missile attacks targeting Bahrain and Kuwait in response to U.S. airstrikes that hit the Islamic Republic.

video:video.video:title: 'I'm at least somewhat like Mark Twain': Bill Maher receives Twain humor prize
video:video.video:description: The Mark Twain Prize for American Humor honors individuals who have made significant contributions to comedy and social commentary.

video:video.video:title: Europe's record heat has overwhelmed Paris mortuaries and left families in distress
video:video.video:description: Public Health France said there were more than 1,200 deaths last Wednesday, when France registered its hottest-ever day.

video:video.video:title: Meet members of Albania's 'Flamingo Revolution' trying to torpedo Kushner-linked development
video:video.video:description: Albania’s rallies against a coastal development project have garnered global attention, both for their connection to U.S.
